problems.label: 1a
problems.display: The value of a certain number of five-cent coins is 485 cents. How many coins?

problems.label: 1b
problems.display: The total cost of 25 buckets is $112.50. How much does one bucket cost?

problems.label: 1c
problems.display: Ann is 16 1/2 years younger than Elizabeth. When Elizabeth is 89, how old will Ann be?

problems.label: 1d
problems.display: The area of a square is 169 square centimetres. How long is its side?

problems.label: 1e
problems.display: Two adjacent angles make a right angle. One angle is 23°. What does the other angle measure?

problems.label: 2a
problems.display: 15 friends shared the cost of party supplies equally. Each share was $13. What was the total cost?

problems.label: 2b
problems.display: Erica bought a computer for $380.16 total. Sales tax was $28.16. What was the price before tax?

problems.label: 2c
problems.display: Henry bought 15 litres of gasoline for $23.25. How much was the price per litre?

problems.label: 2d
problems.display: The perimeter of a rectangle is 1 1/2 m, and one side is 1/4 m. How long is the other side?

problems.label: 2e
problems.display: Three adjacent angles make a straight angle (180°). Two are 88° and 25°. What is the third?

problems.group: 3. Calculate the price of a 3-year-old automobile.
problems.display: Calculate the price of a 3-year-old automobile using New Price = 0.76 · 0.94² · p for each selling price (see workbook p. 81)

problems.group: 4. Challenge: Find the original selling price.
problems.display: (Challenge) If a 3-year car costs $27,398.67, what was the original selling price?

problems.label: 5a
problems.display: Mystery expression: I am the product of 8 and a certain sum. Distributing gives 8b + 24. What am I?

problems.label: 5b
problems.display: Mystery expression: Multiply me by 3 then by 2, and you get 6t − 18s + 30. What am I?
